name: async-form
description: The <form> companion to use-form: submits through the form, surfaces the form-level error as a role=alert, and moves focus to the first invalid field after a failed submit.

notes: The form-level submit error is a role=alert, announced when it appears
After a failed submit, focus moves to the first invalid field (or the form error)
Native browser validation is disabled (noValidate) so the custom announcements own it

intents: an accessible form that focuses the first invalid field on submit
wire a useForm to a <form> with a form-level error alert
submit a form with pending and error handling
examples: <AsyncForm form={form} aria-label="Sign up"><Field label="Email" error={form.errors.email}><input {...form.register('email')} /></Field><AsyncButton type="submit" disabled={form.submitting}>Sign up</AsyncButton></AsyncForm>
